# Copilot instructions for adaptco/agent-shell

This file helps future GitHub Copilot CLI sessions understand how to build, test, lint, and navigate this repository.

---

Build, test, and lint commands

- Setup workspace (repo-provided scripts):
  - PowerShell (Windows):
    Set-Location "path\to\agent-shell-service" && .\scripts\setup_workspace.ps1
  - Bash (Linux/macOS/container):
    cd path/to/agent-shell && ./scripts/setup_workspace.sh

- Install the package ( editable ):
  - py -m pip install -e .[test]
  - or use the repo bootstrap scripts which enforce .venv and Node v24

- Run tests (full suite):
  - .venv\Scripts\pytest --cov=runtime
  - or from repo root when package installed: pytest -q

- Run a single test or test function:
  - .venv\Scripts\pytest tests\path\to\test_file.py::test_function_name -q
  - or: pytest tests/path/to/test_file.py::test_function_name -q

- Linting / formatting:
  - Ruff is configured in pyproject.toml. Run: ruff .
  - Settings: line-length 120, select E,F (ignore E501). isort is configured via ruff settings.

- Local server / entrypoints:
  - CLI: agent-shell (runtime.cli:main)
  - API server CLI: agent-shell-api (runtime.server:main)
  - Direct uvicorn: uvicorn runtime.api:create_app --factory --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000

- External CI status publisher:
  - agent-shell-external-ci --repo adaptco/agent-shell --context external-ci --command "pytest -q"
  - Requires GitHub App credentials (GITHUB_APP_ID, GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH or GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY)

---

High-level architecture (big picture)

- Core package: runtime/
  - Runtime kernel: file-backed task queue, worker loop, tool dispatch, receipts and state stores.
  - Tools and plugins: built-in tool registry (file_read, bash, web_search) and extension points for MCP/desktop plugins.
  - Entry points: runtime.cli, runtime.server, runtime.external_ci.

- Service surface:
  - FastAPI app exposes endpoints (health, tasks, run, heartbeat). The API wraps the runtime kernel and provides gateway/auth boundaries.

- Deployment/operations:
  - External CI status publisher (GitHub App) for non-Actions CI runs.
  - .github/workflows contain CI and release workflows.

- Documentation & contracts:
  - RUNTIME_HARNESS_CONTRACTS.md defines tool/plugin contracts and audit model.
  - IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md and ENHANCEMENT_SUMMARY.md contain phased implementation and architecture decisions.

---

Key conventions and repo-specific patterns

- Python version: Minimum 3.13 (pyproject.toml & GEMINI.md mandate).
- Local venv: Use .venv in repo; setup scripts create/activate it.
- Package installed editable during development (pip install -e .[test]).
- Tests live under tests/ and pytest is configured via pyproject.toml (testpaths = ["tests"]).
- Ruff is the chosen linter. Check pyproject.toml for selected rules (ruff target py313, line-length 120).
- Entrypoint names are authoritative: agent-shell, agent-shell-api, agent-shell-external-ci.
- Node.js v24 is required for Node tooling; repository contains .nvmrc/.node-version to pin it.
- External CI: uses a GitHub App; required env vars are documented in README.md.
- Audit receipts: tool executions generate immutable receipts (see RUNTIME_HARNESS_CONTRACTS.md) — important when adding tooling or tests that inspect receipts.
- Long-form design documents exist (RUNTIME_HARNESS_CONTRACTS.md, IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md, ENHANCEMENT_SUMMARY.md). Copilot responses should prefer these as authoritative sources for protocol/contract details.

---

AI assistant / other tooling files discovered

- .github/workflows/* contain CI and release workflows (test.yml, release.yml, deploy.yml, self-heal.yml).
- GEMINI.md contains project mandates (Python >=3.13, .venv, pytest command, runtime package location).

---

What to do when using Copilot CLI in this repo

- Read pyproject.toml first to discover test/lint configuration and entry points.
- Prefer runtime/ files and the RUNTIME_HARNESS_CONTRACTS.md for protocol details rather than individual implementation files.
- When suggesting changes that affect CI or releases, reference .github/workflows and the external-ci publisher requirements.
- If asked to run tests or lint, use the repository setup scripts to ensure correct Python and Node versions.

---

Relevant file pointers (authoritative quick links)

- pyproject.toml -> project configuration, scripts, pytest and ruff settings
- README.md / QUICK_START.md -> setup and common commands
- RUNTIME_HARNESS_CONTRACTS.md -> tool/plugin contracts, audit model
- IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md -> step-by-step implementation phases


Generated by Copilot CLI analysis.
